message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily due to the way water absorbs and scatters sunlight. When sunlight enters the ocean, water molecules absorb colors at the red end of the light spectrum more effectively than those at the blue end. As a result, blue wavelengths are scattered and reflected back to our eyes, making the ocean look blue. Additionally, factors such as the presence of algae, sediment, and other substances can influence the water's color, but the intrinsic properties of water are the main reason for its striking blue
